changes_made: Renamed k8s_node_autoscaler_enabled to k8s_cluster_autoscaler_enabled for consistency with Kubernetes terminology
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes and align with the NIST SC-6 control for resource availability. Pod Disruption Budgets, Cluster Autoscalers, and High Availability configurations are relevant to ensuring resource availability in a Kubernetes environment. The naming convention mostly follows the established pattern, but a minor change was made for consistency.
naming_improvements: Use 'cluster' instead of 'node' for autoscaler to reflect the broader scope of the autoscaler in Kubernetes
title: Resource Availability
step1_reasoning: The control SC-6 focuses on resource availability, which in Kubernetes can be addressed by ensuring that workloads are resilient to failures and that the cluster can scale to meet demand. Pod Disruption Budgets (PDBs) ensure that a minimum number of pods are always available, while Node Autoscalers and High Availability configurations ensure that the cluster can handle increased load and failover scenarios.
